'''田植踊'''（たうえおどり） *[[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[太白区]]秋保町に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[秋保の田植踊]]を参照。 *[[岩手県]][[紫波郡]][[紫波町]]山屋に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[山屋の田植踊]]を参照。 *[[福島県]][[二本松市]]石東町・錦町・北トロミに伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[石井の七福神と田植踊]]を参照。
{{aimai}}

revision.text:
'''田植踊'''（たうえおどり） *[[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[太白区]]秋保町に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[秋保の田植踊]]を参照。 *[[岩手県]][[紫波郡]][[紫波町]]山屋に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[山屋の田植踊]]を参照。 *[[福島県]][[二本松市]]石東町・錦町・北トロミに伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[石井の七福神と田植踊]]を参照。
{{aimai}} [[Category:日本の伝統芸能|たうえおどり]]

'''田植踊'''（たうえおどり） *[[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[太白区]]秋保町に伝わる[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]][[無形文化遺産]]の民俗芸能。[[秋保の田植踊]]を参照。 *宮城県[[黒川郡]][[富谷町]]富谷に伝わる[[宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|宮城県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[富谷田植踊り]]を参照。 *[[岩手県]][[紫波郡]][[紫波町]]山屋に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[山屋の田植踊]]を参照。 *岩手県[[岩手郡]][[滝沢村]]大沢に伝わる滝沢村の[[無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[大沢田植踊り]]を参照。 *[[山形県]][[東根市]]小田島に伝わる東根市の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[小田島田植踊り]]を参照。 *[[福島県]][[二本松市]]石東町・錦町・北トロミに伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[石井の七福神と田植踊]]を参照。 *福島県[[双葉郡]][[浪江町]]津島に伝わる福島県の無形文化財の民俗芸能。[[津島の田植踊]]を参照。 *福島県[[本宮市]]稲沢字梅ケ久保に伝わる本宮市の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[稲沢御田植踊り]]を参照。 *[[青森県]][[八戸市|八戸]]地方に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[八戸のえんぶり]]を参照。
{{aimai}} [[Category:日本の伝統芸能|たうえおどり]]

'''田植踊'''（たうえおどり） *[[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[太白区]]秋保町に伝わる[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]][[無形文化遺産]]の民俗芸能。[[秋保の田植踊]]を参照。 *宮城県[[黒川郡]][[富谷町]]富谷に伝わる[[宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|宮城県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[富谷田植踊り]]を参照。 *[[岩手県]][[紫波郡]][[紫波町]]山屋に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[山屋の田植踊]]を参照。 *岩手県[[岩手郡]][[滝沢村]]大沢に伝わる滝沢村の[[無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[大沢田植踊り]]を参照。 *[[山形県]][[東根市]]小田島に伝わる東根市の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[小田島田植踊り]]を参照。 *[[福島県]][[二本松市]]石東町・錦町・北トロミに伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[石井の七福神と田植踊]]を参照。 *福島県[[双葉郡]][[浪江町]]津島に伝わる福島県の無形文化財の民俗芸能。[[津島の田植踊]]を参照。 *福島県[[本宮市]]稲沢字梅ケ久保に伝わる本宮市の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[稲沢御田植踊り]]を参照。 *[[青森県]][[八戸市|八戸]]地方に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[八戸のえんぶり]]を参照。
{{aimai}} {{デフォルトソート:たうえおとり}} [[Category:日本の伝統芸能]]

'''田植踊'''（たうえおどり）は、主に[[宮城県|宮城]]・[[岩手県|岩手]]両県を中心にした[[東北地方]]で[[小正月]]のころに行われる[[予祝芸能]]、[[田遊び]]が[[風流踊|風流化]]したものとされる。
== 概要 == 田遊びのように模擬的に[[稲作]]の様子を演じる[[儀礼]]とは異なり、田植踊は[[芸能]]性が顕著である。[[村]]の[[青年]]や[[少年]][[少女]]によって行われ、弥十郎などと称される音頭取りとともに[[早乙女]]が[[花笠]]を付けて[[民家]]を廻り、弥十郎の口上の後田植え歌に合わせて踊るものが多い。
[[神社]]や[[寺院]]に[[奉納]]してから家々を巡る例もあり、場所によっては[[田の神]]や[[七福神]]が登場して[[新年]]を寿ぐ例もあるなど細部には異同がある。
== 代表的な田植踊 == * [[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[太白区]]秋保町に伝わる[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]][[無形文化遺産]]の民俗芸能、[[秋保の田植踊]]。 * 宮城県[[黒川郡]][[富谷町]]富谷に伝わる[[宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|宮城県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[富谷田植踊り]]。 * [[岩手県]][[紫波郡]][[紫波町]]山屋に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[山屋の田植踊]]。 * 岩手県[[岩手郡]][[滝沢村]]大沢に伝わる滝沢村の[[無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[大沢田植踊り]]。 * [[山形県]][[東根市]]小田島に伝わる東根市の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[小田島田植踊り]]。 * [[福島県]][[二本松市]]石東町・錦町・北トロミに伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[石井の七福神と田植踊]]。 * 福島県[[双葉郡]][[浪江町]]津島に伝わる福島県の無形文化財の民俗芸能、[[津島の田植踊]]。 * 福島県[[本宮市]]稲沢字梅ケ久保に伝わる本宮市の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[稲沢御田植踊り]]。 * [[青森県]][[八戸市|八戸]]地方に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[八戸のえんぶり]]。
== 参考文献 == * 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7、1066頁
{{日本の伝統芸能}}
{{デフォルトソート:たうえおとり}} [[Category:稲作]] [[Category:日本の祭り]] [[Category:予祝芸能]] {{Event-stub}}

== 概要 == 田遊びのように模擬的に[[稲作]]の様子を演じる[[儀礼]]とは異なり、田植踊は[[芸能]]性が顕著である。[[村]]の[[青年]]や[[少年]][[少女]]によって行われ、弥十郎などと称される音頭取りとともに早乙女が[[花笠]]を付けて[[民家]]を廻り、弥十郎の口上の後田植え歌に合わせて踊るものが多い。
[[神社]]や[[寺院]]に[[奉納]]してから家々を巡る例もあり、場所によっては[[田の神]]や[[七福神]]が登場して[[新年]]を寿ぐ例もあるなど細部には異同がある。
== 代表的な田植踊 == * [[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[太白区]]秋保町に伝わる[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]][[無形文化遺産]]の民俗芸能、[[秋保の田植踊]]。 * 宮城県仙台市[[青葉区 (仙台市)|青葉区]]愛子に伝わる[[宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|宮城県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[愛子の田植踊]]。 * 宮城県[[黒川郡]][[富谷市]]富谷に伝わる宮城県の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[富谷田植踊り]]。 * [[岩手県]][[紫波郡]][[紫波町]]山屋に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[山屋の田植踊]]。 * 岩手県[[滝沢市]]大沢に伝わる同市の[[無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[大沢田植踊り]]。 * [[山形県]][[東根市]]小田島に伝わる同市の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[小田島田植踊り]]。 * [[福島県]][[二本松市]]石東町・錦町・北トロミに伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[石井の七福神と田植踊]]。 * 福島県[[双葉郡]][[浪江町]]津島に伝わる福島県の無形文化財の民俗芸能、[[津島の田植踊]]。 * 福島県[[本宮市]]稲沢字梅ケ久保に伝わる同市の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[稲沢御田植踊り]]。 * [[青森県]][[八戸市|八戸]]地方に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[八戸のえんぶり]]。
== 参考文献 == * 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7、1066頁

== 概要 == 田遊びのように模擬的に[[稲作]]の様子を演じる[[儀礼]]とは異なり、田植踊は[[芸能]]性が顕著である。[[村]]の[[青年]]や[[少年]][[少女]]によって行われ、弥十郎などと称される音頭取りとともに早乙女が[[花笠]]を付けて[[民家]]を廻り、弥十郎の口上の後田植え歌に合わせて踊るものが多い。

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|rice-field gods|ja|田の神}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akihisa Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|rice-field gods|ja|田の神}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akihisa Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|rice-field gods|ja|田の神}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akihisa Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|rice-field gods|ja|田の神}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe revision.comment: trying to fix the botcher interwiki
revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|rice-field gods|ja|田の神}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|rice-field gods|ja|田の神}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|rice-field gods|ja|田の神}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|rice-field gods|ja|田の神}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|rice-field gods|ja|田の神}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

[[Category:Rice cultivation]] [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:Yoshuku performing arts]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Rice-planting dance]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Rice-planting dance]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Rice-planting dance]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Rice-planting dance]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Rice-planting dance]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:田植踊]]

'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|rice-field gods|ja|田の神}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

{{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

{{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:qq-" to "[[Category:qq deprecated start]]<!--"
revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

{{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.

'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.

* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

{{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}},
