'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|風流踊|" to "|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|"
revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=draft}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方|12=draft}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月|12=draft}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=draft}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=draft}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=draft}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=draft}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年|12=draft}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年|12=draft}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女|12=draft}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=draft}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅|12=draft}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納|12=draft}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社|12=draft}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院|12=draft}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods|12=draft}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神|12=draft}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年|12=draft}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊|12=draft}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=draft}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町|12=draft}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Aiko Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=draft}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=draft}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=draft}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区|12=draft}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=draft}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市|12=draft}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=draft}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|12=draft}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|12=draft}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|12=draft}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=draft}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=draft}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡|12=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=draft}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|12=draft}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市|12=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Emburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|12=draft}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市|12=draft}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|12=draft}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.text:
'''田植踊'''（たうえおどり）は、主に[[宮城県|宮城]]・[[岩手県|岩手]]両県を中心にした[[東北地方]]で[[小正月]]のころに行われる[[予祝芸能]]、[[田遊び]]が[[風流踊|風流化]]したものとされる。
== 概要 == 田遊びのように模擬的に[[稲作]]の様子を演じる[[儀礼]]とは異なり、田植踊は[[芸能]]性が顕著である。[[村]]の[[青年]]や[[少年]][[少女]]によって行われ、弥十郎などと称される音頭取りとともに早乙女が[[花笠]]を付けて[[住宅|民家]]を廻り、弥十郎の口上の後田植え歌に合わせて踊るものが多い。
[[神社]]や[[寺院]]に[[奉納]]してから家々を巡る例もあり、場所によっては[[田の神]]や[[七福神]]が登場して[[新年]]を寿ぐ例もあるなど細部には異同がある。
== 代表的な田植踊 == * [[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[太白区]]秋保町に伝わる[[国際連合教育科学文化機関|ユネスコ]][[無形文化遺産]]の[[民俗芸能]]、[[秋保の田植踊]]。 * 宮城県仙台市[[青葉区 (仙台市)|青葉区]]愛子に伝わる[[宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|宮城県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[愛子の田植踊]]。 * 宮城県[[黒川郡]][[富谷市]]富谷に伝わる宮城県の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[富谷田植踊り]]。 * [[岩手県]][[紫波郡]][[紫波町]]山屋に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[山屋の田植踊]]。 * 岩手県[[滝沢市]]大沢に伝わる同市の[[無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[大沢田植踊り]]。 * [[山形県]][[東根市]]小田島に伝わる同市の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[小田島田植踊り]]。 * [[福島県]][[二本松市]]石東町・錦町・北トロミに伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[石井の七福神と田植踊]]。 * 福島県[[双葉郡]][[浪江町]]津島に伝わる福島県の無形文化財の民俗芸能、[[津島の田植踊]]。 * 福島県[[本宮市]]稲沢字梅ケ久保に伝わる同市の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[稲沢御田植踊り]]。 * [[青森県]][[八戸市|八戸]]地方に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[八戸のえんぶり]]。
== 参考文献 == * 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7、1066頁
{{日本の伝統芸能}} {{Event-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:たうえおとり}} [[Category:稲作]] [[Category:日本の祭り]] [[Category:予祝芸能]]

== 参考文献 == * 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7、1066頁

It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.

[[sn:Rice-planting dance]] [[ja:田植踊]] [[en:Rice-planting dance]]
<!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance-->
==Merged second translation== '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|celebration entertainment|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celebration entertainment|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}} that evolved into a {{ill|furyu odori|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}, predominantly performed around {{ill|Koshogatsu|ja|小正月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshogatsu|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|qq=}}, especially in Miyagi and Iwate prefectures.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which involves ceremonial reenactments of {{ill|rice farming|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice farming|qq=}}, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong entertainment aspects. The performances are typically carried out by village {{ill|youths|ja|青年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/youths|qq=}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boys|qq=}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/girls|qq=}}, accompanied by a leader referred to as "Yajuro." Participants, often dressed as early rice planters wearing {{ill|flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flower hats|qq=}}, visit private homes. Following a speech by Yajuro, they dance to rice planting songs.
Some performances begin with dedications at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrines|qq=}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples|qq=}} before visiting homes. Variations exist depending on location, including the inclusion of {{ill|rice field gods|ja|田の神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice field gods|qq=}} or {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Lucky Gods|qq=}} to celebrate the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year|qq=}}.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu Taue Odori|qq=}}: A UNESCO {{ill|Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performance from {{ill|Akiu Town|ja|秋保町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku Ward|qq=}}, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba Ward|qq=}}, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tomiya Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Tomiya|ja|富谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya|qq=}}, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|山屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiwa Town|ja|紫波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Town|qq=}}, Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Osawa|ja|大沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Takizawa City|ja|滝沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takizawa City|qq=}}, Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Odajima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Odajima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima|qq=}}, {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashine City|qq=}}, Yamagata Prefecture. * {{ill|Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|qq=}}, {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu City|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tsunami Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Fukushima Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Tsunami|ja|津島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami|qq=}}, {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namie Town|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Taue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomiya City|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from the {{ill|Hachinohe region|ja|八戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe region|qq=}}, Aomori Prefecture.
== References == * Asakura Shoten, ed. (2005). "郷土史大辞典 下" (Encyclopedia of Local History, Volume 2). ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|qq=}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Small New Year|qq=}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|qq=}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|qq=}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|qq=}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/youth|qq=}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boys|qq=}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/girls|qq=}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|qq=}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/households|qq=}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dedication|qq=}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrines|qq=}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples|qq=}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Lucky Gods|qq=}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year|qq=}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akihisa|qq=}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai City|qq=}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|qq=}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba Ward|qq=}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama|qq=}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|qq=}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namie Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Futaba [[sn:Rice-planting dance]] [[ja:田植踊]] [[en:Rice-planting dance]]
<!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance-->
==Merged second translation== '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|celebration entertainment|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celebration entertainment|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}} that evolved into a {{ill|furyu odori|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}, predominantly performed around {{ill|Koshogatsu|ja|小正月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshogatsu|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|qq=}}, especially in Miyagi and Iwate prefectures.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which involves ceremonial reenactments of {{ill|rice farming|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice farming|qq=}}, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong entertainment aspects. The performances are typically carried out by village {{ill|youths|ja|青年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/youths|qq=}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boys|qq=}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/girls|qq=}}, accompanied by a leader referred to as "Yajuro." Participants, often dressed as early rice planters wearing {{ill|flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flower hats|qq=}}, visit private homes. Following a speech by Yajuro, they dance to rice planting songs.
Some performances begin with dedications at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrines|qq=}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples|qq=}} before visiting homes. Variations exist depending on location, including the inclusion of {{ill|rice field gods|ja|田の神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice field gods|qq=}} or {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Lucky Gods|qq=}} to celebrate the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year|qq=}}.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu Taue Odori|qq=}}: A UNESCO {{ill|Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performance from {{ill|Akiu Town|ja|秋保町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku Ward|qq=}}, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba Ward|qq=}}, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tomiya Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Tomiya|ja|富谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya|qq=}}, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|山屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiwa Town|ja|紫波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Town|qq=}}, Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Osawa|ja|大沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Takizawa City|ja|滝沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takizawa City|qq=}}, Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Odajima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Odajima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima|qq=}}, {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashine City|qq=}}, Yamagata Prefecture. * {{ill|Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|qq=}}, {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu City|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tsunami Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Fukushima Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Tsunami|ja|津島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami|qq=}}, {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namie Town|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Taue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomiya City|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from the {{ill|Hachinohe region|ja|八戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe region|qq=}}, Aomori Prefecture.
== References == * Asakura Shoten, ed. (2005). "郷土史大辞典 下" (Encyclopedia of Local History, Volume 2). ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|qq=}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Small New Year|qq=}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|qq=}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|qq=}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|qq=}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/youth|qq=}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boys|qq=}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/girls|qq=}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|qq=}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/households|qq=}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dedication|qq=}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrines|qq=}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples|qq=}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Lucky Gods|qq=}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year|qq=}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akihisa|qq=}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai City|qq=}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|qq=}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba Ward|qq=}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama|qq=}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|qq=}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Region|qq=}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Prefecture|qq=}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

[[sn:Rice-planting dance]] [[ja:田植踊]] [[en:Rice-planting dance]]
<!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance-->
==Merged second translation== '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|celebration entertainment|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celebration entertainment|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}} that evolved into a {{ill|furyu odori|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}, predominantly performed around {{ill|Koshogatsu|ja|小正月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshogatsu|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|qq=}}, especially in Miyagi and Iwate prefectures.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which involves ceremonial reenactments of {{ill|rice farming|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice farming|qq=}}, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong entertainment aspects. The performances are typically carried out by village {{ill|youths|ja|青年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/youths|qq=}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boys|qq=}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/girls|qq=}}, accompanied by a leader referred to as "Yajuro." Participants, often dressed as early rice planters wearing {{ill|flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flower hats|qq=}}, visit private homes. Following a speech by Yajuro, they dance to rice planting songs.
Some performances begin with dedications at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrines|qq=}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples|qq=}} before visiting homes. Variations exist depending on location, including the inclusion of {{ill|rice field gods|ja|田の神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice field gods|qq=}} or {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Lucky Gods|qq=}} to celebrate the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year|qq=}}.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu Taue Odori|qq=}}: A UNESCO {{ill|Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performance from {{ill|Akiu Town|ja|秋保町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku Ward|qq=}}, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba Ward|qq=}}, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tomiya Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Tomiya|ja|富谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya|qq=}}, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|山屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiwa Town|ja|紫波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Town|qq=}}, Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Osawa|ja|大沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Takizawa City|ja|滝沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takizawa City|qq=}}, Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Odajima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Odajima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima|qq=}}, {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashine City|qq=}}, Yamagata Prefecture. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|qq=}}, {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu City|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tsunami Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Fukushima Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Tsunami|ja|津島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami|qq=}}, {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namie Town|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Taue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomiya City|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from the {{ill|Hachinohe region|ja|八戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe region|qq=}}, Aomori Prefecture.
== References == * Asakura Shoten, ed. (2005). "郷土史大辞典 下" (Encyclopedia of Local History, Volume 2). ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|qq=}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Small New Year|qq=}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|qq=}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|qq=}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|qq=}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/youth|qq=}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boys|qq=}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/girls|qq=}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|qq=}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/households|qq=}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dedication|qq=}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrines|qq=}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples|qq=}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Lucky Gods|qq=}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year|qq=}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akihisa|qq=}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai City|qq=}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|qq=}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba Ward|qq=}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama|qq=}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|qq=}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashine City|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|qq=}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiki District|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|qq=}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namie Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaba District|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomiya City|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Region|qq=}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Prefecture|qq=}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

Participants, often dressed as early rice planters wearing {{ill|flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flower hats|qq=}}, visit private homes. Following a speech by Yajuro, they dance to rice planting songs.
Some performances begin with dedications at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrines|qq=}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples|qq=}} before visiting homes. Variations exist depending on location, including the inclusion of {{ill|rice field gods|ja|田の神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice field gods|qq=}} or {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Lucky Gods|qq=}} to celebrate the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year|qq=}}.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu Taue Odori|qq=}}: A UNESCO {{ill|Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performance from {{ill|Akiu Town|ja|秋保町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku Ward|qq=}}, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba Ward|qq=}}, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tomiya Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Tomiya|ja|富谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya|qq=}}, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|山屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiwa Town|ja|紫波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Town|qq=}}, Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Osawa|ja|大沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Takizawa City|ja|滝沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takizawa City|qq=}}, Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Odajima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Odajima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima|qq=}}, {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashine City|qq=}}, Yamagata Prefecture. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|qq=}}, {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu City|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Fukushima Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Tsunami|ja|津島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami|qq=}}, {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namie Town|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Taue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomiya City|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from the {{ill|Hachinohe region|ja|八戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe region|qq=}}, Aomori Prefecture.
