revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|qq=}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|10=ja|11=宮城県|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Small New Year|qq=}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|qq=}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|qq=}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|qq=}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/youth|qq=}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boys|qq=}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/girls|qq=}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|qq=}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/households|qq=}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dedication|qq=}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrines|qq=}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples|qq=}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Lucky Gods|qq=}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year|qq=}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akihisa|qq=}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai City|qq=}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|10=ja|11=宮城県|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|qq=}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba Ward|qq=}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama|qq=}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|10=ja|11=宮城県|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa District|qq=}}, Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}. * Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Region|qq=}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Prefecture|qq=}}.
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

[[sn:Rice-planting dance]] [[ja:田植踊]] [[en:Rice-planting dance]]
<!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance-->
==Merged second translation== '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Celebration entertainment|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celebration entertainment|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}} that evolved into a {{ill|furyu odori|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}, predominantly performed around {{ill|Koshogatsu|ja|小正月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshogatsu|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|qq=}}, especially in Miyagi and Iwate prefectures.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which involves ceremonial reenactments of {{ill|Rice Cultivation|lt=Rice Cultivation|lt=Rice farming|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice farming|qq=}}, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong entertainment aspects. The performances are typically carried out by village {{ill|youths|ja|青年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/youths|qq=}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boys|qq=}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/girls|qq=}}, accompanied by a leader referred to as "Yajuro." Participants, often dressed as early rice planters wearing {{ill|Hanagasa|lt=Hanagasa|lt=Flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flower hats|qq=}}, visit private homes. Following a speech by Yajuro, they dance to rice planting songs.
Some performances begin with dedications at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrines|qq=}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples|qq=}} before visiting homes. Variations exist depending on location, including the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|lt=Ta-no-Kami|lt=Rice field gods|ja|田の神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice field gods|qq=}} or {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Lucky Gods|qq=}} to celebrate the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year|qq=}}.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu Taue Odori|qq=}}: A UNESCO {{ill|Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performance from {{ill|Akiu Town|ja|秋保町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku Ward|qq=}}, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba Ward|qq=}}, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tomiya Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Tomiya|ja|富谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya|qq=}}, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|山屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiwa Town|ja|紫波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Town|qq=}}, Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Osawa|ja|大沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Takizawa City|ja|滝沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takizawa City|qq=}}, Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Odajima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Odajima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima|qq=}}, {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashine City|qq=}}, Yamagata Prefecture. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|qq=}}, {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu City|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Fukushima Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Tsunami|ja|津島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami|qq=}}, {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namie Town|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Taue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomiya City|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from the {{ill|Hachinohe region|ja|八戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe region|qq=}}, Aomori Prefecture.
== References == * Asakura Shoten, ed. (2005). "郷土史大辞典 下" (Encyclopedia of Local History, Volume 2). ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tohoku region|ja|東北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku region|qq=}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|10=ja|11=宮城県|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}, during the {{ill|Small New Year|ja|小正月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Small New Year|qq=}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|qq=}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|qq=}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|qq=}} {{ill|youth|ja|青年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/youth|qq=}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boys|qq=}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/girls|qq=}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|qq=}}. Performers visit {{ill|households|ja|住宅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/households|qq=}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|dedication|ja|奉納|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dedication|qq=}} at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrines|qq=}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples|qq=}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神|Ta-no-Kami=Ta-no-Kami|lt=rice-field gods|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Lucky Gods|qq=}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year|qq=}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akihisa|ja|秋保町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akihisa|qq=}} in {{ill|Sendai City|ja|仙台市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai City|qq=}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|10=ja|11=宮城県|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|qq=}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}} in {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba Ward|qq=}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiyama|ja|富谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama|qq=}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|10=ja|11=宮城県|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|紫波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=}} in {{ill|Shiwa District|ja|紫波郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|滝沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|qq=}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine City|ja|東根市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashine City|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|qq=}}, {{ill|Nishiki District|ja|錦町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiki District|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|qq=}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie Town|ja|浪江町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namie Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Futaba District|ja|双葉郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaba District|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Motomiya City|ja|本宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomiya City|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe Region|ja|八戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Region|qq=}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Prefecture|qq=}}.

== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which involves ceremonial reenactments of {{ill|Rice Cultivation|lt=Rice Cultivation|lt=Rice farming|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice farming|qq=}}, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong entertainment aspects. The performances are typically carried out by village {{ill|youths|ja|青年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/youths|qq=}}, {{ill|boys|ja|少年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/boys|qq=}}, and {{ill|girls|ja|少女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/girls|qq=}}, accompanied by a leader referred to as "Yajuro." Participants, often dressed as early rice planters wearing {{ill|Hanagasa|lt=Hanagasa|lt=Flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flower hats|qq=}}, visit private homes. Following a speech by Yajuro, they dance to rice planting songs.
Some performances begin with dedications at {{ill|shrines|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrines|qq=}} or {{ill|temples|ja|寺院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples|qq=}} before visiting homes. Variations exist depending on location, including the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|lt=Ta-no-Kami|lt=Rice field gods|ja|田の神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice field gods|qq=}} or {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|ja|七福神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Lucky Gods|qq=}} to celebrate the {{ill|New Year|ja|新年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year|qq=}}.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu Taue Odori|ja|秋保の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu Taue Odori|qq=}}: A UNESCO {{ill|Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performance from {{ill|Akiu Town|ja|秋保町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Taihaku Ward|ja|太白区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku Ward|qq=}}, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Aoba Ward|ja|青葉区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba Ward|qq=}}, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tomiya Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Tomiya|ja|富谷市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya|qq=}}, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|山屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiwa Town|ja|紫波町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Town|qq=}}, Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Osawa|ja|大沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Takizawa City|ja|滝沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takizawa City|qq=}}, Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Odajima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|qq=}}, {{ill|Nihonmatsu City|ja|二本松市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku region]] of Japan, particularly in [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] and [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], during the [[:en:First Full Moon Festival|Small New Year]] season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|qq=}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|qq=}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|qq=}} [[:en:Youth|youth]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|qq=}}. Performers visit [[:en:Dwelling|households]], performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a [[:en:Votive offering|dedication]] at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice-field gods]] and [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], celebrating the [[:en:New Year|New Year]] in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu no Taue Odori]]: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performing art from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akihisa]] in [[:en:Sendai|Sendai City]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|qq=}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}} in [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiyama]], designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Yamaya]] in [[:en:Shiwa District, Iwate|Shiwa District]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|qq=}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|qq=}}, [[:en:Nishiki, Kumamoto|Nishiki District]], and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|qq=}} in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], [[:en:Futaba District, Fukushima|Futaba District]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from the [[:en:Hachinohe|Hachinohe Region]] in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

==Merged second translation== '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Celebration entertainment|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celebration entertainment|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}} that evolved into a {{ill|furyu odori|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}, predominantly performed around [[:en:First Full Moon Festival|Koshogatsu]] in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku region]], especially in Miyagi and Iwate prefectures.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which involves ceremonial reenactments of {{ill|Rice Cultivation|lt=Rice Cultivation|lt=Rice farming|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice farming|qq=}}, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong entertainment aspects. The performances are typically carried out by village [[:en:Youth|youths]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader referred to as "Yajuro." Participants, often dressed as early rice planters wearing {{ill|Hanagasa|lt=Hanagasa|lt=Flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flower hats|qq=}}, visit private homes. Following a speech by Yajuro, they dance to rice planting songs.
Some performances begin with dedications at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist depending on location, including the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Rice field gods]] or [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]] to celebrate the [[:en:New Year|New Year]].
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu Taue Odori]]: A UNESCO [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible Cultural Heritage]] folk performance from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akiu Town]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}}, [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tomiya Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiya]], Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|山屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=}}, [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Shiwa Town]], Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Osawa|ja|大沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa|qq=}}, [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takizawa City]], Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Odajima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Odajima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima|qq=}}, [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], Yamagata Prefecture. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|qq=}} [[:en:Youth|youth]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|qq=}}. Performers visit [[:en:Dwelling|households]], performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a [[:en:Votive offering|dedication]] at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice-field gods]] and [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], celebrating the [[:en:New Year|New Year]] in unique ways.

* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu no Taue Odori]]: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performing art from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akihisa]] in [[:en:Sendai|Sendai City]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ayashi [[sn:Rice-planting dance]] [[ja:田植踊]] [[en:Rice-planting dance]]
<!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance-->
==Merged second translation== '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Celebration entertainment|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celebration entertainment|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}} that evolved into a {{ill|furyu odori|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}, predominantly performed around [[:en:First Full Moon Festival|Koshogatsu]] in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku region]], especially in Miyagi and Iwate prefectures.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which involves ceremonial reenactments of {{ill|Rice Cultivation|lt=Rice Cultivation|lt=Rice farming|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice farming|qq=}}, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong entertainment aspects. The performances are typically carried out by village [[:en:Youth|youths]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader referred to as "Yajuro." Participants, often dressed as early rice planters wearing {{ill|Hanagasa|lt=Hanagasa|lt=Flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flower hats|qq=}}, visit private homes. Following a speech by Yajuro, they dance to rice planting songs.
Some performances begin with dedications at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist depending on location, including the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Rice field gods]] or [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]] to celebrate the [[:en:New Year|New Year]].
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu Taue Odori]]: A UNESCO [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible Cultural Heritage]] folk performance from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akiu Town]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}}, [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tomiya Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiya]], Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|山屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=}}, [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Shiwa Town]], Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Osawa|ja|大沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa|qq=}}, [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takizawa City]], Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Odajima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Odajima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima|qq=}}, [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], Yamagata Prefecture. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|qq=}}, [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Fukushima Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from [[:en:Tsushima|Tsunami]], [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Taue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from the [[:en:Hachinohe|Hachinohe region]], Aomori Prefecture.
== References == * Asakura Shoten, ed. (2005). "郷土史大辞典 下" (Encyclopedia of Local History, Volume 2). ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku region]] of Japan, particularly in [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] and [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], during the [[:en:First Full Moon Festival|Small New Year]] season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|qq=}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|qq=}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|qq=}} [[:en:Youth|youth]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|qq=}}. Performers visit [[:en:Dwelling|households]], performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a [[:en:Votive offering|dedication]] at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice-field gods]] and [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], celebrating the [[:en:New Year|New Year]] in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu no Taue Odori]]: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performing art from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akihisa]] in [[:en:Sendai|Sendai City]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|qq=}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}} in [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiyama]], designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Yamaya]] in [[:en:Shiwa District, Iwate|Shiwa District]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|qq=}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|qq=}}, [[:en:Nishiki, Kumamoto|Nishiki District]], and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|qq=}} in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], [[:en:Futaba District, Fukushima|Futaba District]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from the [[:en:Hachinohe|Hachinohe Region]] in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku region]] of Japan, particularly in [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] and [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], during the [[:en:First Full Moon Festival|Small New Year]] season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|qq=}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|qq=}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|qq=}} [[:en:Youth|youth]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|qq=}}. Performers visit [[:en:Dwelling|households]], performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a [[:en:Votive offering|dedication]] at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice-field gods]] and [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], celebrating the [[:en:New Year|New Year]] in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu no Taue Odori]]: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=}} folk performing art from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akihisa]] in [[:en:Sendai|Sendai City]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|qq=}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}} in [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiyama]], designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Yamaya]] in [[:en:Shiwa District, Iwate|Shiwa District]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|qq=}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|qq=}}, [[:en:Nishiki, Kumamoto|Nishiki District]], and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|qq=}} in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], [[:en:Futaba District, Fukushima|Futaba District]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from the [[:en:Hachinohe|Hachinohe Region]] in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

<!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance-->
==Merged second translation== '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Celebration entertainment|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celebration entertainment|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}} that evolved into a {{ill|furyu odori|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}, predominantly performed around [[:en:First Full Moon Festival|Koshogatsu]] in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku region]], especially in Miyagi and Iwate prefectures.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which involves ceremonial reenactments of {{ill|Rice Cultivation|lt=Rice Cultivation|lt=Rice farming|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice farming|qq=}}, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong entertainment aspects. The performances are typically carried out by village [[:en:Youth|youths]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader referred to as "Yajuro." Participants, often dressed as early rice planters wearing {{ill|Hanagasa|lt=Hanagasa|lt=Flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flower hats|qq=}}, visit private homes. Following a speech by Yajuro, they dance to rice planting songs.
Some performances begin with dedications at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist depending on location, including the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Rice field gods]] or [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]] to celebrate the [[:en:New Year|New Year]].
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu Taue Odori]]: A UNESCO [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible Cultural Heritage]] folk performance from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akiu Town]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}}, [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tomiya Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiya]], Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|山屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=}}, [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Shiwa Town]], Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Osawa|ja|大沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa|qq=}}, [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takizawa City]], Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Odajima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Odajima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima|qq=}}, [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], Yamagata Prefecture. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|qq=}}, [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Fukushima Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from [[:en:Tsushima|Tsunami]], [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Taue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Taue Odori|qq=}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from the [[:en:Hachinohe|Hachinohe region]], Aomori Prefecture.
== References == * Asakura Shoten, ed. (2005). "郷土史大辞典 下" (Encyclopedia of Local History, Volume 2). ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

[[sn:Rice-planting dance]] [[ja:田植踊]]
<!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance--><!--Rice-planting dance-->
==Merged second translation== '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Celebration entertainment|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celebration entertainment|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}} that evolved into a {{ill|furyu odori|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}, predominantly performed around [[:en:First Full Moon Festival|Koshogatsu]] in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku region]], especially in Miyagi and Iwate prefectures.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which involves ceremonial reenactments of {{ill|Rice Cultivation|lt=Rice Cultivation|lt=Rice farming|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice farming|qq=}}, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong entertainment aspects. The performances are typically carried out by village [[:en:Youth|youths]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader referred to as "Yajuro." Participants, often dressed as early rice planters wearing {{ill|Hanagasa|lt=Hanagasa|lt=Flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flower hats|qq=}}, visit private homes. Following a speech by Yajuro, they dance to rice planting songs.
Some performances begin with dedications at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist depending on location, including the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|Rice field gods]] or [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]] to celebrate the [[:en:New Year|New Year]].
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu Taue Odori]]: A UNESCO [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible Cultural Heritage]] folk performance from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akiu Town]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}}, [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tomiya Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya Taue Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiya]], Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|山屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Shiwa Town]], Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Osawa|ja|大沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa|qq=}}, [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takizawa City]], Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Odajima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima Taue Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Odajima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima|qq=}}, [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], Yamagata Prefecture. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|qq=}}, [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Fukushima Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from [[:en:Tsushima|Tsunami]], [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Taue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Taue Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from the [[:en:Hachinohe|Hachinohe region]], Aomori Prefecture.
== References == * Asakura Shoten, ed. (2005). "郷土史大辞典 下" (Encyclopedia of Local History, Volume 2). ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku region]] of Japan, particularly in [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] and [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], during the [[:en:First Full Moon Festival|Small New Year]] season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|qq=}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|qq=}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} [[:en:Youth|youth]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|qq=}}. Performers visit [[:en:Dwelling|households]], performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a [[:en:Votive offering|dedication]] at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice-field gods]] and [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], celebrating the [[:en:New Year|New Year]] in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu no Taue Odori]]: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akihisa]] in [[:en:Sendai|Sendai City]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|qq=}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}} in [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiyama]], designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Yamaya]] in [[:en:Shiwa District, Iwate|Shiwa District]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|qq=}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nishiki, Kumamoto|Nishiki District]], and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], [[:en:Futaba District, Fukushima|Futaba District]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from the [[:en:Hachinohe|Hachinohe Region]] in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].

* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.text:
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku region]] of Japan, particularly in [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] and [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], during the [[:en:First Full Moon Festival|Small New Year]] season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyu-odori|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyu-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|qq=}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|qq=}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} [[:en:Youth|youth]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|qq=}}. Performers visit [[:en:Dwelling|households]], performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a [[:en:Votive offering|dedication]] at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice-field gods]] and [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], celebrating the [[:en:New Year|New Year]] in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu no Taue Odori]]: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産}} folk performing art from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akihisa]] in [[:en:Sendai|Sendai City]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|qq=}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|qq=}} in [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiyama]], designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Yamaya]] in [[:en:Shiwa District, Iwate|Shiwa District]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|qq=}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nishiki, Kumamoto|Nishiki District]], and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], [[:en:Futaba District, Fukushima|Futaba District]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|qq=}} from the [[:en:Hachinohe|Hachinohe Region]] in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.
