revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11577057]] Wikidata name: taue odori [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku region]] of Japan, particularly in [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] and [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], during the [[:en:First Full Moon Festival|Small New Year]] season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=rice farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=village of Japan|WD=Q4174776}} [[:en:Youth|youth]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit [[:en:Dwelling|households]], performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a [[:en:Votive offering|dedication]] at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice-field gods]] and [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], celebrating the [[:en:New Year|New Year]] in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu no Taue Odori]]: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akihisa]] in [[:en:Sendai|Sendai City]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453703}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Aiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}} in [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiyama]], designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Yamaya]] in [[:en:Shiwa District, Iwate|Shiwa District]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|1=Kodashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463027}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nishiki, Kumamoto|Nishiki District]], and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], [[:en:Futaba District, Fukushima|Futaba District]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from the [[:en:Hachinohe|Hachinohe Region]] in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu Taue Odori]]: A UNESCO [[:en:Intangible cultural heritage|Intangible Cultural Heritage]] folk performance from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akiu Town]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Aiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}}, [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tomiya Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiya Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A Miyagi Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiya]], Miyagi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Yamaya|ja|山屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Shiwa Town]], Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Osawa|ja|大沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa|1=Osawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118164641}}, [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takizawa City]], Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Odajima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Odajima|ja|小田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima|1=Odajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463027}}, [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], Yamagata Prefecture. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|1=Ishii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585007}}, [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Fukushima Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from [[:en:Tsushima|Tsunami]], [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Taue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from the [[:en:Hachinohe|Hachinohe region]], Aomori Prefecture.

[[ja:田植踊]] [[Category:Pre-celebration Performing Arts]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11577057}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11577057]] Wikidata name: taue odori [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku region]] of Japan, particularly in [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] and [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], during the [[:en:First Full Moon Festival|Small New Year]] season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=rice farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=village of Japan|WD=Q4174776}} [[:en:Youth|youth]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit [[:en:Dwelling|households]], performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a [[:en:Votive offering|dedication]] at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice-field gods]] and [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], celebrating the [[:en:New Year|New Year]] in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu no Taue Odori]]: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akihisa]] in [[:en:Sendai|Sendai City]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453703}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Aiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}} in [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiyama]], designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Yamaya]] in [[:en:Shiwa District, Iwate|Shiwa District]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|1=Kodashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463027}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nishiki, Kumamoto|Nishiki District]], and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], [[:en:Futaba District, Fukushima|Futaba District]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from the [[:en:Hachinohe|Hachinohe Region]] in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

cultural heritage of [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nishiki, Kumamoto|Nishiki District]], and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], [[:en:Futaba District, Fukushima|Futaba District]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11577057]] Wikidata name: taue odori [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku region]] of Japan, particularly in [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] and [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], during the [[:en:First Full Moon Festival|Small New Year]] season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=rice farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=village of Japan|WD=Q4174776}} [[:en:Youth|youth]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit [[:en:Dwelling|households]], performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a [[:en:Votive offering|dedication]] at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice-field gods]] and [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], celebrating the [[:en:New Year|New Year]] in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu no Taue Odori]]: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akihisa]] in [[:en:Sendai|Sendai City]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453703}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Aiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}} in [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiyama]], designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Yamaya]] in [[:en:Shiwa District, Iwate|Shiwa District]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|1=Kodashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463027}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nishiki, Kumamoto|Nishiki District]], and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], [[:en:Futaba District, Fukushima|Futaba District]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from the [[:en:Hachinohe|Hachinohe Region]] in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=rice farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=village of Japan|WD=Q4174776}} [[:en:Youth|youth]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit [[:en:Dwelling|households]], performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a [[:en:Votive offering|dedication]] at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice-field gods]] and [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], celebrating the [[:en:New Year|New Year]] in unique ways.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11577057]] Wikidata name: taue odori [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku region]] of Japan, particularly in [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] and [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], during the [[:en:First Full Moon Festival|Small New Year]] season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=rice farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=village of Japan|WD=Q4174776}} [[:en:Youth|youth]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit [[:en:Dwelling|households]], performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a [[:en:Votive offering|dedication]] at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice-field gods]] and [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], celebrating the [[:en:New Year|New Year]] in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu no Taue Odori]]: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akihisa]] in [[:en:Sendai|Sendai City]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453703}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Aiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}} in [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiyama]], designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Yamaya]] in [[:en:Shiwa District, Iwate|Shiwa District]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|1=Kodashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463027}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nishiki, Kumamoto|Nishiki District]], and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], [[:en:Futaba District, Fukushima|Futaba District]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11577057]] Wikidata name: taue odori [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku region]] of Japan, particularly in [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]] and [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], during the [[:en:First Full Moon Festival|Small New Year]] season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=rice farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=village of Japan|WD=Q4174776}} [[:en:Youth|youth]], [[:en:Boy|boys]], and [[:en:Girl|girls]], accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit [[:en:Dwelling|households]], performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a [[:en:Votive offering|dedication]] at [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] or [[:en:Buddhist temple|temples]] before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice-field gods]] and [[:en:Seven Lucky Gods|Seven Lucky Gods]], celebrating the [[:en:New Year|New Year]] in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* [[:en:Akiu no Taue Odori|Akiu no Taue Odori]]: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from [[:en:Akiu, Miyagi|Akihisa]] in [[:en:Sendai|Sendai City]], [[:en:Taihaku-ku, Sendai|Taihaku Ward]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453703}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Aiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}} in [[:en:Aoba-ku|Aoba Ward]], Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from [[:en:Tomiya, Miyagi|Tomiyama]], designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from [[:en:Shiwa, Iwate|Yamaya]] in [[:en:Shiwa District, Iwate|Shiwa District]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|1=Kodashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463027}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Higashine|Higashine City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nishiki, Kumamoto|Nishiki District]], and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], [[:en:Futaba District, Fukushima|Futaba District]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from the [[:en:Hachinohe|Hachinohe Region]] in [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]].
== References ==
* 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005th year ）ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Fukushima Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from [[:en:Tsushima|Tsunami]], [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Taue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Motomiya, Fukushima|Motomiya City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from the [[:en:Hachinohe|Hachinohe region]], Aomori Prefecture.
== References == * Asakura Shoten, ed. (2005). "郷土史大辞典 下" (Encyclopedia of Local History, Volume 2). ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

{{ill|Osawa|ja|大沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa|1=Osawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118164641}}, [[:en:Takizawa, Iwate|Takizawa City]], Iwate Prefecture. * {{ill|Odajima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A local intangible cultural property from Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|1=Ishii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585007}}, [[:en:Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|Nihonmatsu City]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Fukushima Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from [[:en:Tsushima|Tsunami]], [[:en:Namie, Fukushima|Namie Town]], Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Taue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A local intangible cultural revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11577057]] Wikidata name: taue odori [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku region}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}, during the {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|WD=Q13423452|lt=Small New Year}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=rice farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=village of Japan|WD=Q4174776}} {{ill|Youth|en|Youth|WD=Q190007|lt=youth}}, {{ill|Boy|en|Boy|WD=Q3010|lt=boys}}, and {{ill|Girl|en|Girl|WD=Q3031|lt=girls}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit {{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=households}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|Votive offering|en|Votive offering|WD=Q2601788|lt=dedication}} at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines}} or {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temples}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|en|Akiu no Taue Odori|WD=Q2829083|lt=Akiu no Taue Odori}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akiu, Miyagi|en|Akiu, Miyagi|WD=Q4701253|lt=Akihisa}} in {{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|WD=Q46747|lt=Sendai City}}, {{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|WD=Q1043331|lt=Taihaku Ward}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453703}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Aiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}} in {{ill|Aoba-ku|en|Aoba-ku|WD=Q904456|lt=Aoba Ward}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiya, Miyagi|en|Tomiya, Miyagi|WD=Q1205173|lt=Tomiyama}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Shiwa, Iwate|en|Shiwa, Iwate|WD=Q387336|lt=Yamaya}} in {{ill|Shiwa District, Iwate|en|Shiwa District, Iwate|WD=Q1131886|lt=Shiwa District}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takizawa, Iwate|en|Takizawa, Iwate|WD=Q1205932|lt=Takasaki City}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|1=Kodashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463027}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine|en|Higashine|WD=Q823927|lt=Higashine City}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishiki, Kumamoto|en|Nishiki, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184741|lt=Nishiki District}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|en|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|WD=Q685491|lt=Nihonmatsu City}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie, Fukushima|en|Namie, Fukushima|WD=Q1023937|lt=Namie Town}}, {{ill|Futaba District, Fukushima|en|Futaba District, Fukushima|WD=Q1167153|lt=Futaba District}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Motomiya, Fukushima|en|Motomiya, Fukushima|WD=Q867106|lt=Motomiya City}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe|en|Hachinohe|WD=Q191914|lt=Hachinohe Region}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture}}.

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11577057}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pre-celebration Performing Arts]] [[Category:Rice production]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11577057]] Wikidata name: taue odori [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku region}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}, during the {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|WD=Q13423452|lt=Small New Year}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=rice farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=village of Japan|WD=Q4174776}} {{ill|Youth|en|Youth|WD=Q190007|lt=youth}}, {{ill|Boy|en|Boy|WD=Q3010|lt=boys}}, and {{ill|Girl|en|Girl|WD=Q3031|lt=girls}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit {{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=households}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|Votive offering|en|Votive offering|WD=Q2601788|lt=dedication}} at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines}} or {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temples}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|en|Akiu no Taue Odori|WD=Q2829083|lt=Akiu no Taue Odori}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akiu, Miyagi|en|Akiu, Miyagi|WD=Q4701253|lt=Akihisa}} in {{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|WD=Q46747|lt=Sendai City}}, {{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|WD=Q1043331|lt=Taihaku Ward}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based Prefecture. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Fukushima|WD=Q685491|lt=Nihonmatsu City}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Fukushima Prefectural Intangible Folk revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]]
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku region}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}, during the {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|WD=Q13423452|lt=Small New Year}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=rice farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=village of Japan|WD=Q4174776}} {{ill|Youth|en|Youth|WD=Q190007|lt=youth}}, {{ill|Boy|en|Boy|WD=Q3010|lt=boys}}, and {{ill|Girl|en|Girl|WD=Q3031|lt=girls}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit {{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=households}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|Votive offering|en|Votive offering|WD=Q2601788|lt=dedication}} at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines}} or {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temples}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|en|Akiu no Taue Odori|WD=Q2829083|lt=Akiu no Taue Odori}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akiu, Miyagi|en|Akiu, Miyagi|WD=Q4701253|lt=Akihisa}} in {{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|WD=Q46747|lt=Sendai City}}, {{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|WD=Q1043331|lt=Taihaku Ward}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453703}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Aiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}} in {{ill|Aoba-ku|en|Aoba-ku|WD=Q904456|lt=Aoba Ward}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiya, Miyagi|en|Tomiya, Miyagi|WD=Q1205173|lt=Tomiyama}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Shiwa, Iwate|en|Shiwa, Iwate|WD=Q387336|lt=Yamaya}} in {{ill|Shiwa District, Iwate|en|Shiwa District, Iwate|WD=Q1131886|lt=Shiwa District}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takizawa, Iwate|en|Takizawa, Iwate|WD=Q1205932|lt=Takasaki City}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|1=Kodashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463027}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine|en|Higashine|WD=Q823927|lt=Higashine City}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishiki, Kumamoto|en|Nishiki, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184741|lt=Nishiki District}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|en|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|WD=Q685491|lt=Nihonmatsu City}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie, Fukushima|en|Namie, Fukushima|WD=Q1023937|lt=Namie Town}}, {{ill|Futaba District, Fukushima|en|Futaba District, Fukushima|WD=Q1167153|lt=Futaba District}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Motomiya, Fukushima|en|Motomiya, Fukushima|WD=Q867106|lt=Motomiya City}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe|en|Hachinohe|WD=Q191914|lt=Hachinohe Region}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture}}.
== References ==

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]]
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku region}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}, during the {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|WD=Q13423452|lt=Small New Year}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=rice farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=village of Japan|WD=Q4174776}} {{ill|Youth|en|Youth|WD=Q190007|lt=youth}}, {{ill|Boy|en|Boy|WD=Q3010|lt=boys}}, and {{ill|Girl|en|Girl|WD=Q3031|lt=girls}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit {{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=households}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|Votive offering|en|Votive offering|WD=Q2601788|lt=dedication}} at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines}} or {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temples}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|en|Akiu no Taue Odori|WD=Q2829083|lt=Akiu no Taue Odori}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akiu, Miyagi|en|Akiu, Miyagi|WD=Q4701253|lt=Akihisa}} in {{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|WD=Q46747|lt=Sendai City}}, {{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|WD=Q1043331|lt=Taihaku Ward}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453703}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Aiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}} in {{ill|Aoba-ku|en|Aoba-ku|WD=Q904456|lt=Aoba Ward}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiya, Miyagi|en|Tomiya, Miyagi|WD=Q1205173|lt=Tomiyama}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Cultural Property from {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|1=Ishii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585007}}, {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|en|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|WD=Q685491|lt=Nihonmatsu City}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Fukushima Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Tsushima|en|Tsushima|WD=Q52048|lt=Tsunami}}, {{ill|Namie, Fukushima|en|Namie, Fukushima|WD=Q1023937|lt=Namie Town}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Taue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A local intangible cultural property from revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]]
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku region}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}, during the {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|WD=Q13423452|lt=Small New Year}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=rice farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=village of Japan|WD=Q4174776}} {{ill|Youth|en|Youth|WD=Q190007|lt=youth}}, {{ill|Boy|en|Boy|WD=Q3010|lt=boys}}, and {{ill|Girl|en|Girl|WD=Q3031|lt=girls}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit {{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=households}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|Votive offering|en|Votive offering|WD=Q2601788|lt=dedication}} at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines}} or {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temples}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|en|Akiu no Taue Odori|WD=Q2829083|lt=Akiu no Taue Odori}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akiu, Miyagi|en|Akiu, Miyagi|WD=Q4701253|lt=Akihisa}} in {{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|WD=Q46747|lt=Sendai City}}, {{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|WD=Q1043331|lt=Taihaku Ward}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453703}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Aiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}} in {{ill|Aoba-ku|en|Aoba-ku|WD=Q904456|lt=Aoba Ward}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiya, Miyagi|en|Tomiya, Miyagi|WD=Q1205173|lt=Tomiyama}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Shiwa, Iwate|en|Shiwa, Iwate|WD=Q387336|lt=Yamaya}} in {{ill|Shiwa District, Iwate|en|Shiwa District, Iwate|WD=Q1131886|lt=Shiwa District}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takizawa, Iwate|en|Takizawa, Iwate|WD=Q1205932|lt=Takasaki City}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|1=Kodashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463027}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine|en|Higashine|WD=Q823927|lt=Higashine City}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishiki, Kumamoto|en|Nishiki, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184741|lt=Nishiki District}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|en|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|WD=Q685491|lt=Nihonmatsu City}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie, Fukushima|en|Namie, Fukushima|WD=Q1023937|lt=Namie Town}}, {{ill|Futaba District, Fukushima|en|Futaba District, Fukushima|WD=Q1167153|lt=Futaba District}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Motomiya, Fukushima|en|Motomiya, Fukushima|WD=Q867106|lt=Motomiya City}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe|en|Hachinohe|WD=Q191914|lt=Hachinohe Region}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture}}.

'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku region}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}, during the {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|WD=Q13423452|lt=Small New Year}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.

Performers visit {{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=households}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|Votive offering|en|Votive offering|WD=Q2601788|lt=dedication}} at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines}} or {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temples}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice-field gods}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year}} in unique ways.
