revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]]
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}, during the {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|WD=Q13423452|lt=Small New Year|1=First Full Moon Festival}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=Rice Cultivation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Village (Japan)|WD=Q4174776}} {{ill|Youth|en|Youth|WD=Q190007|lt=youth|1=Youth}}, {{ill|Boy|en|Boy|WD=Q3010|lt=boys|1=Boy}}, and {{ill|Girl|en|Girl|WD=Q3031|lt=girls|1=Girl}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=Hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit {{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=households|1=Dwelling}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|Votive offering|en|Votive offering|WD=Q2601788|lt=dedication|1=Votive offering}} at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} or {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temples|1=Buddhist temple}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice-field gods|1=Ta-no-Kami}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods|1=Seven Lucky Gods}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year|1=New Year}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|en|Akiu no Taue Odori|WD=Q2829083|lt=Akiu no Taue Odori|1=Akiu no Taue Odori}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=Intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akiu, Miyagi|en|Akiu, Miyagi|WD=Q4701253|lt=Akihisa|1=Akiu, Miyagi}} in {{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|WD=Q46747|lt=Sendai City|1=Sendai}}, {{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|WD=Q1043331|lt=Taihaku Ward|1=Taihaku-ku, Sendai}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453703}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Q118130530|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}} in {{ill|Aoba-ku|en|Aoba-ku|WD=Q904456|lt=Aoba Ward|1=Aoba-ku}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiya, Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine|en|Higashine|WD=Q823927|lt=Higashine City|1=Higashine}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata from {{ill|Odajima|ja|小田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odajima|1=Q11463027|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463027}}, {{ill|Higashine|en|Higashine|WD=Q823927|lt=Higashine City|1=Higashine}}, Yamagata Prefecture. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii’s Seven Lucky Gods and Taue Odori|qq=}}: A nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|1=Ishii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585007}}, {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|en|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|WD=Q685491|lt=Nihonmatsu City|1=Nihonmatsu, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunami Taue Odori|qq=}}: A Fukushima Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property from {{ill|Tsushima|en|Tsushima|WD=Q52048|lt=Tsunami|1=Tsushima}}, {{ill|Namie, Fukushima|en|Namie, Fukushima|WD=Q1023937|lt=Namie Town|1=Namie, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Taue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]]
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}, during the {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|WD=Q13423452|lt=Small New Year|1=First Full Moon Festival}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=Rice Cultivation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Village (Japan)|WD=Q4174776}} {{ill|Youth|en|Youth|WD=Q190007|lt=youth|1=Youth}}, {{ill|Boy|en|Boy|WD=Q3010|lt=boys|1=Boy}}, and {{ill|Girl|en|Girl|WD=Q3031|lt=girls|1=Girl}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=Hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit {{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=households|1=Dwelling}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|Votive offering|en|Votive offering|WD=Q2601788|lt=dedication|1=Votive offering}} at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} or {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temples|1=Buddhist temple}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice-field gods|1=Ta-no-Kami}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods|1=Seven Lucky Gods}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year|1=New Year}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|en|Akiu no Taue Odori|WD=Q2829083|lt=Akiu no Taue Odori|1=Akiu no Taue Odori}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=Intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akiu, Miyagi|en|Akiu, Miyagi|WD=Q4701253|lt=Akihisa|1=Akiu, Miyagi}} in {{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|WD=Q46747|lt=Sendai City|1=Sendai}}, {{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|WD=Q1043331|lt=Taihaku Ward|1=Taihaku-ku, Sendai}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453703}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Q118130530|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}} in {{ill|Aoba-ku|en|Aoba-ku|WD=Q904456|lt=Aoba Ward|1=Aoba-ku}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiya, Miyagi|en|Tomiya, Miyagi|WD=Q1205173|lt=Tomiyama|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Shiwa, Iwate|en|Shiwa, Iwate|WD=Q387336|lt=Yamaya|1=Shiwa, Iwate}} in {{ill|Shiwa District, Iwate|en|Shiwa District, Iwate|WD=Q1131886|lt=Shiwa District|1=Shiwa District, Iwate}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takizawa, Iwate|en|Takizawa, Iwate|WD=Q1205932|lt=Takasaki City|1=Takizawa, Iwate}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|1=Q11463027|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463027}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine|en|Higashine|WD=Q823927|lt=Higashine City|1=Higashine}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishiki, Kumamoto|en|Nishiki, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184741|lt=Nishiki District|1=Nishiki, Kumamoto}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|en|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|WD=Q685491|lt=Nihonmatsu City|1=Nihonmatsu, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie, Fukushima|en|Namie, Fukushima|WD=Q1023937|lt=Namie Town|1=Namie, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Futaba District, Fukushima|en|Futaba District, Fukushima|WD=Q1167153|lt=Futaba District|1=Futaba District, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Motomiya, Fukushima|en|Motomiya, Fukushima|WD=Q867106|lt=Motomiya City|1=Motomiya, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe|en|Hachinohe|WD=Q191914|lt=Hachinohe Region|1=Hachinohe}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}.
== References ==

Performers visit {{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=households|1=Dwelling}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|Votive offering|en|Votive offering|WD=Q2601788|lt=dedication|1=Votive offering}} at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} or {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temples|1=Buddhist temple}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice-field gods|1=Ta-no-Kami}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods|1=Seven Lucky Gods}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year|1=New Year}} in unique ways.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]]
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}, during the {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|WD=Q13423452|lt=Small New Year|1=First Full Moon Festival}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=Rice Cultivation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Village (Japan)|WD=Q4174776}} {{ill|Youth|en|Youth|WD=Q190007|lt=youth|1=Youth}}, {{ill|Boy|en|Boy|WD=Q3010|lt=boys|1=Boy}}, and {{ill|Girl|en|Girl|WD=Q3031|lt=girls|1=Girl}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=Hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit {{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=households|1=Dwelling}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|Votive offering|en|Votive offering|WD=Q2601788|lt=dedication|1=Votive offering}} at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} or {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temples|1=Buddhist temple}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice-field gods|1=Ta-no-Kami}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods|1=Seven Lucky Gods}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year|1=New Year}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==

Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=Intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akiu, Ward|1=Taihaku-ku, Sendai}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]]
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}, during the {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|WD=Q13423452|lt=Small New Year|1=First Full Moon Festival}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=Rice Cultivation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Village (Japan)|WD=Q4174776}} {{ill|Youth|en|Youth|WD=Q190007|lt=youth|1=Youth}}, {{ill|Boy|en|Boy|WD=Q3010|lt=boys|1=Boy}}, and {{ill|Girl|en|Girl|WD=Q3031|lt=girls|1=Girl}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=Hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit {{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=households|1=Dwelling}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|Votive offering|en|Votive offering|WD=Q2601788|lt=dedication|1=Votive offering}} at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} or {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temples|1=Buddhist temple}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice-field gods|1=Ta-no-Kami}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods|1=Seven Lucky Gods}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year|1=New Year}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|en|Akiu no Taue Odori|WD=Q2829083|lt=Akiu no Taue Odori|1=Akiu no Taue Odori}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=Intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akiu, Miyagi|en|Akiu, Miyagi|WD=Q4701253|lt=Akihisa|1=Akiu, Miyagi}} in {{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|WD=Q46747|lt=Sendai City|1=Sendai}}, {{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|WD=Q1043331|lt=Taihaku Ward|1=Taihaku-ku, Sendai}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453703}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Q118130530|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}} in {{ill|Aoba-ku|en|Aoba-ku|WD=Q904456|lt=Aoba Ward|1=Aoba-ku}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiya, Miyagi|en|Tomiya, Miyagi|WD=Q1205173|lt=Tomiyama|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Shiwa, Iwate|en|Shiwa, Iwate|WD=Q387336|lt=Yamaya|1=Shiwa, Iwate}} in {{ill|Shiwa District, Iwate|en|Shiwa District, Iwate|WD=Q1131886|lt=Shiwa District|1=Shiwa District, Iwate}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takizawa, Iwate|en|Takizawa, Iwate|WD=Q1205932|lt=Takasaki City|1=Takizawa, Iwate}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|1=Q11463027|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463027}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine|en|Higashine|WD=Q823927|lt=Higashine City|1=Higashine}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishiki, Kumamoto|en|Nishiki, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184741|lt=Nishiki District|1=Nishiki, Kumamoto}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|en|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|WD=Q685491|lt=Nihonmatsu City|1=Nihonmatsu, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie, Fukushima|en|Namie, Fukushima|WD=Q1023937|lt=Namie Town|1=Namie, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Futaba District, Fukushima|en|Futaba District, Fukushima|WD=Q1167153|lt=Futaba District|1=Futaba District, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Motomiya, Fukushima|en|Motomiya, Fukushima|WD=Q867106|lt=Motomiya City|1=Motomiya, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe '''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}, during the {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|WD=Q13423452|lt=Small New Year|1=First Full Moon Festival}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=Rice Cultivation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Village (Japan)|WD=Q4174776}} {{ill|Youth|en|Youth|WD=Q190007|lt=youth|1=Youth}}, {{ill|Boy|en|Boy|WD=Q3010|lt=boys|1=Boy}}, and {{ill|Girl|en|Girl|WD=Q3031|lt=girls|1=Girl}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=Hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit {{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=households|1=Dwelling}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|Votive offering|en|Votive offering|WD=Q2601788|lt=dedication|1=Votive offering}} at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} or {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temples|1=Buddhist temple}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice-field gods|1=Ta-no-Kami}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods|1=Seven Lucky Gods}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year|1=New Year}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|en|Akiu no Taue Odori|WD=Q2829083|lt=Akiu no Taue Odori|1=Akiu no Taue Odori}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=Intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akiu, Miyagi|en|Akiu, Miyagi|WD=Q4701253|lt=Akihisa|1=Akiu, Miyagi}} in {{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|WD=Q46747|lt=Sendai City|1=Sendai}}, {{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|WD=Q1043331|lt=Taihaku Ward|1=Taihaku-ku, Sendai}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453703}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Q118130530|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}} in {{ill|Aoba-ku|en|Aoba-ku|WD=Q904456|lt=Aoba Ward|1=Aoba-ku}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiya, Miyagi|en|Tomiya, Miyagi|WD=Q1205173|lt=Tomiyama|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Shiwa, Iwate|en|Shiwa, Iwate|WD=Q387336|lt=Yamaya|1=Shiwa, Iwate}} in {{ill|Shiwa District, Iwate|en|Shiwa District, Iwate|WD=Q1131886|lt=Shiwa District|1=Shiwa District, Iwate}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Osawa Taue Odori|ja|大沢田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osawa Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Takizawa, Iwate|en|Takizawa, Iwate|WD=Q1205932|lt=Takasaki City|1=Takizawa, Iwate}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Kodashima Taue Odori|ja|小田島田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Kodashima|ja|小田島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodashima|1=Q11463027|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463027}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Higashine|en|Higashine|WD=Q823927|lt=Higashine City|1=Higashine}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from {{ill|Ishi District|ja|石東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nishiki, Kumamoto|en|Nishiki, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184741|lt=Nishiki District|1=Nishiki, Kumamoto}}, and {{ill|Kita Toromi|ja|北トロミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita Toromi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|en|Nihonmatsu, Fukushima|WD=Q685491|lt=Nihonmatsu City|1=Nihonmatsu, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Tsubame Taue Odori|ja|津島の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubame Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Namie, Fukushima|en|Namie, Fukushima|WD=Q1023937|lt=Namie Town|1=Namie, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Futaba District, Fukushima|en|Futaba District, Fukushima|WD=Q1167153|lt=Futaba District|1=Futaba District, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja|稲沢御田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa Otaue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢字梅ケ久保|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Motomiya, Fukushima|en|Motomiya, Fukushima|WD=Q867106|lt=Motomiya City|1=Motomiya, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Asset|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} from the {{ill|Hachinohe|en|Hachinohe|WD=Q191914|lt=Hachinohe Region|1=Hachinohe}} in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}.
== References ==

A local intangible cultural property from {{ill|Inazawa|ja|稲沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inazawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Motomiya, Fukushima|en|Motomiya, Fukushima|WD=Q867106|lt=Motomiya City|1=Motomiya, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Prefecture.
== References == * Asakura Shoten, ed. (2005). "郷土史大辞典 下" (Encyclopedia of Local History, Volume 2). ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1066.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Taue odori]][[ja:田植踊]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田植踊]]
'''Taue Odori''' (田植踊) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|予祝芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} and {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, primarily performed in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of Japan, particularly in {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} and {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}, during the {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|WD=Q13423452|lt=Small New Year|1=First Full Moon Festival}} season. It is believed to have evolved into a {{ill|furyu dance|ja|風流踊|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/furyu dance|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
Unlike {{ill|Ta-asobi|lt=tanuki-themed ceremonies|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}, which often simulate {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=Rice Cultivation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} through symbolic gestures, Taue Odori is characterized by its strong {{ill|performance art|ja|芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/performance art|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} aspects. It is typically performed by {{ill|village|ja|村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Village (Japan)|WD=Q4174776}} {{ill|Youth|en|Youth|WD=Q190007|lt=youth|1=Youth}}, {{ill|Boy|en|Boy|WD=Q3010|lt=boys|1=Boy}}, and {{ill|Girl|en|Girl|WD=Q3031|lt=girls|1=Girl}}, accompanied by a leader called "Yajuro" and wearing {{ill|decorative flower hats|ja|花笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorative flower hats|1=Hanagasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615624}}. Performers visit {{ill|Dwelling|en|Dwelling|WD=Q699405|lt=households|1=Dwelling}}, performing songs and dances, often following a recitation by Yajuro.
In some cases, the performance begins with a {{ill|Votive offering|en|Votive offering|WD=Q2601788|lt=dedication|1=Votive offering}} at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} or {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temples|1=Buddhist temple}} before visiting homes. Variations exist, such as the inclusion of {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice-field gods|1=Ta-no-Kami}} and {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|WD=Q846858|lt=Seven Lucky Gods|1=Seven Lucky Gods}}, celebrating the {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year|1=New Year}} in unique ways.
== Representative Taue Odori Performances ==
* {{ill|Akiu no Taue Odori|en|Akiu no Taue Odori|WD=Q2829083|lt=Akiu no Taue Odori|1=Akiu no Taue Odori}}: A {{ill|UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja|ユネスコ無形文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 無形文化遺産|1=Intangible cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84036549}} folk performing art from {{ill|Akiu, Miyagi|en|Akiu, Miyagi|WD=Q4701253|lt=Akihisa|1=Akiu, Miyagi}} in {{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|WD=Q46747|lt=Sendai City|1=Sendai}}, {{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|WD=Q1043331|lt=Taihaku Ward|1=Taihaku-ku, Sendai}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ayashi no Taue Odori|ja|愛子の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayashi no Taue Odori|qq=}}: A folk performing art designated as a {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|ja|宮城県指定文化財一覧#無形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture Cultural Heritage|1=list of cultural properties designated by Miyagi prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453703}}, from {{ill|Aiko|ja|愛子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiko|1=Q118130530|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118130530}} in {{ill|Aoba-ku|en|Aoba-ku|WD=Q904456|lt=Aoba Ward|1=Aoba-ku}}, Sendai City. * {{ill|Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja|富谷田植踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiyama Taue Odori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A folk performing art from {{ill|Tomiya, Miyagi|en|Tomiya, Miyagi|WD=Q1205173|lt=Tomiyama|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}}, designated as an intangible cultural heritage of {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Yamaya Taue Odori|ja|山屋の田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaya Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|ja|石井の七福神と田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii no Shichifukujin to Taue Odori|qq=}}: A {{ill|National Important
